frameworks.name: discrete attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: stable states pull memories toward few representations, biasing but reducing noise diffusion

frameworks.name: diffusion / random noise accumulation
frameworks.one_line: internal noise that randomly perturbs memory representations over time

frameworks.name: adaptive context integration
frameworks.one_line: attractors shift toward contextually-predicted values as environmental statistics change

core_question: How does working memory mitigate the accumulation of internal noise in stored representations?
key_assumptions: working memory is corrupted by internal noise over time
errors grow with delay and memory load
memory dynamics can be described by attractor landscapes in mnemonic space
manipulations: memory load (number of simultaneously held items)
statistics of the environment / contextual predictions
key_findings: attractor dynamics account for the distribution, bias, and precision of memory reports in humans and monkeys
attractor strength increases as noise increases with memory load
memories drift toward contextually-predicted values when environmental statistics change
discrete attractors trade bias for reduced random diffusion
interpretations: discrete attractor dynamics serve as an error-correcting mechanism counteracting noise in working memory
attractor structure adapts to integrate contextual statistics into stored memories
